# `tables/` — code-emitted LaTeX tables

**Do not hand-edit these files.** They are generated by `code/` and pulled into
`manuscript.tex` with `\input{}`. To change a table, change the code that emits
it and re-run `python code/run_all.py`.

| File | Emitted by | Pulled into |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| `attrition.tex` | `01_build.py` | Data section (sample construction) |
| `summary_stats.tex` | `02_analyze.py` | Data section (descriptives) |
| `convergence_regressions.tex` | `02_analyze.py` | Results section (main table) |
| `estimates.tex` | `02_analyze.py` | preamble — defines in-text `\newcommand` macros (`\betaAbs`, `\betaCond`, …) so prose never hard-codes a number |

These files **are** committed (so the paper compiles without re-running the
analysis), but they are generated artifacts — treat them as read-only.
